commit.message:
Add origin check to service worker and implement CSP headers (#6)

* Add security hardening: CSP meta tag and scoped SW caching

- Add Content-Security-Policy meta tag to index.html restricting
  script/style/font/image sources to trusted origins only
- Scope service worker caching to same-origin requests only,
  preventing unnecessary caching of cross-origin opaque responses
  which can consume excessive storage in some browsers

https://claude.ai/code/session_01E4HxTqGvKsznevQeKxUzYh

https://claude.ai/code/session_01E4HxTqGvKsznevQeKxUzYh

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix saved-name effect: use Timer→view_effect→Manual sequence

view_effect only activates reliably when the device is already in Timer
mode.  The previous fix (preview_effect) caused saved-name effects to look
identical to their preview counterparts.

New sequence for saved_name effects:
  1. If device is not in Timer mode, set it to Timer first (waits 1s)
  2. Call view_effect(saved_id) — activates the actual device-saved pattern
  3. Set to Manual — locks the effect in place for the alarm duration

This ensures the custom IntruderRedBlueStrobe pattern (or any saved
pattern) is invoked as designed on the device, not approximated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add rodeo, misty, and cork trick classification with 2x scoring

Splits off-axis tricks into three types based on real snowboarding:
- Rodeo: backflip + backside spin (S+E)
- Misty: frontflip + any spin (W+Q/E)
- Cork: all other flip+spin combos (fallback)

All off-axis tricks now score double points.

Implement addition operation (#10)

Add support for adding two or more numbers via the 'mathcli add' command.

- CLI integration with argparse subcommand
- Comprehensive test suite with 9 test cases (100% coverage)
- Supports integers, floats, negative numbers, and multiple arguments

All acceptance criteria met:
- Accepts 2+ numeric arguments
- Returns sum of all numbers
- Handles edge cases (validation, error messages)
- Full test coverage
- Clean code following PEP 8

Closes #3

commit.message:
Fix positional arg parsing: void operator, []string in From, nil-safe MatchString

Three fixes that together make yargs positional argument parsing work:

- void operator now emits jsvalue.NewUndefined() instead of nil.
  JS `void 0` produces undefined, used in `x !== void 0` patterns
  (nullish coalescing). Previously both checks used NewNull(),
  causing argvPromise (undefined) to be returned instead of argv.

- From([]string) converts Go string slices to JSValue arrays.
  regexp.FindStringSubmatch returns []string which was falling through
  to the default case, producing NewString("[--name myname]") instead
  of a proper array. The parser couldn't access match groups.

- MatchString nil-safety: return false for nil JSValue arguments
  instead of panicking on nil pointer dereference.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
